step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the consensus that the H-index is an effective measure. One additional point supporting its use is its robustness against self-citation bias compared to pure citation counts. Additionally, since the H-index requires a minimum threshold of citations per paper, it inherently filters out less impactful works which can sometimes inflate simpler citation metrics.

Answer: C (H-index)
Based on the collective support for the H-index and considering its resistance to certain forms of bias and filtering out lower-quality outputs, I concur that it provides a strong, balanced view of scholarly impact. Adding another layer of validation regarding its resilience against self-citations further strengthens its case.
